title: Nouns & Plurals
description: One apple, two apples! Learn the patterns to make German nouns plural.
explanation: German plurals don't just add an 's' like in English. They have a few different endings like -e, -en, or -er. Sometimes, the vowel gets two dots called an Umlaut (like a -> ä). The good news is that the plural article is ALWAYS 'die'!

tables.title: Common Plural Patterns
tables.note: Tip: Most feminine nouns (die) just add -en or -n in the plural!

deep_notes.title: Always 'die' in plural!
deep_notes.text: Remember: Once you have more than one of something, the article is always 'die'. The boy is 'der Junge', but the boys are 'die Jungen'!

examples.explanation: Auto is an international word, so it just gets an 's'.

examples.german: Wir haben Frauen und Männer hier.
examples.english: We have women and men here.

exercises.question: Was ist der Plural von 'die Frau'?

exercises.hint: Das Wort 'Apfel' bekommt im Plural einen Umlaut.

exercises.question: Ordne Singular und Plural zu!
exercises.explanation: Awesome! You've matched the patterns correctly.

exercises.question: Welches Nomen ändert sich im Plural NICHT (außer dem Artikel)?
exercises.explanation: Words ending in -chen (like Mädchen = girl) don't change at all in the plural, only the article changes (das -> die)!

exercises.question: Ordne den Satz:
exercises.explanation: Kinder (plural of Kind) requires 'die' and plural verb 'haben'.

exercises.hint: Der Plural von 'Mann' bekommt einen Umlaut und -er.

exercises.question: Welcher Plural ist korrekt für 'das Auto'?

exercises.hint: Das ist mehr als ein Tisch.
